items.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis is prone to getting caught on the feet or in a submission after dominating early.
items.quote: BSD's the type of guy to dominate this fight for a couple of minutes until he gets caught on the feet or gets caught in a sub.

items.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis leaves his neck exposed when shooting for takedowns.
items.quote: his neck is all the way out as well when he's shooting for those takedowns.

items.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis is very hittable and fights in a telegraphed manner when pressured with body kicks.
items.quote: BSD is very hittable... It made BSD lowers hands a lot and fight very telegraphed.

items.claim: Paddy Pimblett is highly effective in full mount with his elbow strikes.
items.quote: Excellent in full mount. Beautiful elbows.

items.claim: Both Paddy Pimblett and Benoit Saint-Denis lack defensive soundness and get hit frequently.
items.quote: Both of these guys have durability, but they do get hit a lot. They're not as defensively sound.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has not competed in nearly five years.
items.quote: The guy hasn't fought in almost five years, basically five years.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is coming off a broken ankle injury.
items.quote: He's coming off of that broken ankle injury against Dustin Poirier.

items.claim: Conor McGregor displays poor fight IQ by jumping for guillotines when in desperate positions.
items.quote: he kept on jumping the guillotine when it was like a sign of desperation, a sign of just giving up position.

items.claim: Max Holloway has only been finished twice in his professional career.
items.quote: He's only been finished twice in his entire career, once by sub and once by KO.

items.claim: Max Holloway improves his performance and puzzle-solving ability as a fight progresses.
items.quote: When Max finds his rhythm and figures out the puzzle as the fight goes on, this guy is almost unstoppable.
